title: orchis sambucina var. insularis
intro: Orchis sambucina var. insularis, also known as Insular Spurge-flower, is a rare and endemic orchid species native to the Ionian Islands in Greece. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Insular Spurge-flower include its ability to help reduce inflammation, improve kidney function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for urinary tract infections and other kidney-related issues. Insular Spurge-flower is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the dried roots and rhizomes in hot water, and it is also used in some traditional herbal remedies.
